ucs.title: [LLM] WIRTE (Gaza Cybergang) Office→VBS→regsvr32 Squiblydoo chain (G0090)
ucs.description: Hunts WIRTE's signature initial-access chain: a maldoc lure (Israel/Hamas/diplomatic themed) spawns wscript/cscript which calls regsvr32.exe with /i:http(s) to fetch a remote .sct/scriptlet (Squiblydoo). Kaspersky and Check Point have repeatedly attributed this exact tradecraft to WIRTE/Ashen Lepus since 2018, and it remains in use through their 2024 IronWind/SameCoin operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.process_hash) as hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=regsvr32.exe AND (Processes.process="*/i:http*" OR Processes.process="*/i:https*" OR Processes.process="*scrobj.dll*" OR Processes.process="*scrobj*") AND (Processes.parent_process_name=wscript.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=cscript.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=winword.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=excel.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=outlook.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=powerpnt.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=mshta.exe) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename dest as host
ucs.kql:
// WIRTE / Ashen Lepus (G0090) — Squiblydoo Office→script→regsvr32 chain
let _office_or_script_parents = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","powerpnt.exe","mshta.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// WIRTE pattern: regsvr32 fetching a remote scriptlet (the /i:http payload + scrobj COM object)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/i:http", "/i:https", "scrobj.dll", "scrobj")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_office_or_script_parents)
| extend RemoteSct = extract(@"(?i)/i:(https?://[^\s\"]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentFolder = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteSct,
          SHA256, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// WIRTE / Ashen Lepus (G0090) — Squiblydoo Office→script→regsvr32 chain
let _office_or_script_parents = dynamic(["\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\winword.exe","\\excel.exe","\\outlook.exe","\\powerpnt.exe","\\mshta.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/i:http", "/i:https", "scrobj.dll", "scrobj")
| where ParentProcessName endswith_any (_office_or_script_parents)
| extend RemoteSct = extract(@"(?i)/i:(https?://[^\s\"]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, RemoteSct, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: WIRTE Squiblydoo via Office or Script Host (G0090)
id: 7c2a4d0b-3e51-4b1a-9b5c-9f2c1a4e0a01
status: experimental
description: Detects WIRTE / Ashen Lepus (Gaza Cybergang subgroup) Office-macro→VBS→regsvr32 Squiblydoo chain delivering remote scriptlets.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0090/
  - https://securelist.com/wirte-attack-the-middle-east/100231/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0090
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/i:http'
      - '/i:https'
      - 'scrobj'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  condition: selection_image and selection_cmd and selection_parent
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legacy enterprise software using COM scriptlet registration over HTTP (rare; whitelist by URL/host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Squiblydoo (regsvr32 /i:http … scrobj) parented by Office or wscript/cscript is documented as WIRTE’s primary delivery technique by Kaspersky (2019), S2 Grupo, and Check Point. Generic regsvr32 telemetry has FPs; scoping to remote scriptlet URLs AND Office/script-host parents AND non-machine accounts removes legitimate installer noise and isolates the WIRTE/Gaza Cybergang chain that survives acros

ucs.title: [LLM] WIRTE VBS/JS C2 beacon over non-standard ports to public IPs (G0090)
ucs.description: Hunts WIRTE's distinctive post-exploitation pattern: wscript.exe/cscript.exe launched from %AppData%/%Temp% communicating outbound to public IPs over non-standard HTTP/S ports (T1571), the documented C2 transport for their Havoc/IronWind/LitePower stagers tracked under G0090.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app=wscript.exe OR All_Traffic.app=cscript.exe OR All_Traffic.process_name=wscript.exe OR All_Traffic.process_name=cscript.exe) AND All_Traffic.dest_category!=internal AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=80 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=443 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=53 AND All_Traffic.transport=tcp by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",dest_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",dest_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",dest_ip) | stats count dc(dest_port) as port_count values(dest_port) as ports values(dest_ip) as remote_ips by src user app | where count > 5
ucs.kql:
// WIRTE / Ashen Lepus (G0090) — wscript/cscript egressing on non-standard ports
let _well_known = dynamic([53, 80, 443, 8080, 8443, 587, 465, 993, 995]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
// WIRTE LitePower / IronWind beacons documented on ports like 2096, 8443->but also 2052, 2087, 8880
| where RemotePort !in (_well_known)
// AppData / Temp / Public is where WIRTE’s VBS droppers land
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
| summarize Connections = count(),
            DistinctPorts = dcount(RemotePort),
            DistinctIPs = dcount(RemoteIP),
            Ports = make_set(RemotePort, 25),
            Hosts = make_set(RemoteIP, 25),
            URLs = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            SampleFolder = any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| where Connections >= 5 or DistinctPorts >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// WIRTE / Ashen Lepus (G0090) — wscript/cscript egressing on non-standard ports (ASIM)
let _well_known = dynamic([53, 80, 443, 8080, 8443, 587, 465, 993, 995]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| where isnotempty(DstIpAddr)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
| where DstPortNumber !in (_well_known)
| summarize Connections = count(),
            DistinctPorts = dcount(DstPortNumber),
            DistinctIPs = dcount(DstIpAddr),
            Ports = make_set(DstPortNumber, 25),
            Hosts = make_set(DstIpAddr, 25),
            URLs = make_set(Url, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName
| where Connections >= 5 or DistinctPorts >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: WIRTE's reporting (Kaspersky 2021 LitePower, Check Point 2024 IronWind/SameCoin) consistently shows VBS/JS scripts dropped to AppData beaconing over non-443 HTTPS ports — a deliberate evasion that simultaneously fingerprints the actor. Pairing the script interpreter (their characteristic T1059.005 stager) with non-standard-port public egress (T1571) and AppData/Temp execution path filters out the

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
